title: Umami-Infused Egyptian Condensed Milk Pudding
description: A rich and creamy dessert infused with wok-hei from sesame oil and umami from soy sauce, served in individual mason jars. Egyptian spices and condensed milk balance the savory flavors.

ingredients.name: milk powder
ingredients.notes: or substitute with coconut milk powder

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: or substitute with tamari or coconut aminos

ingredients.name: egg yolks
ingredients.notes: or substitute with aquafaba (liquid from canned chickpeas)

instructions: In a small saucepan, heat the sesame oil over low heat for 5-7 minutes to infuse wok-hei.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the condensed milk, granulated sugar, sesame seeds, cocoa powder, milk powder, ground cinnamon, ground black pepper, and soy sauce until well combined.
Temper the egg yolks by slowly pouring the warm sesame oil mixture into the egg yolks while whisking constantly.
Return the saucepan to low heat and cook the egg yolk mixture for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly, until it thickens like a custard.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into individual mason jars.
Allow the pudding to cool to room temperature before refrigerating for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Garnish with additional sesame seeds and serve chilled.
